epigraph.text: The two greatest motivating forces in the human condition are fear and vanity.

follyOfTheDay.headline: ACADEMIA'S PUNCTUATION PURGE: University Bars Author for 'Aggressive' Periods
follyOfTheDay.summary: A major university disinvited a celebrated novelist after discovering he had committed the grave offense of using periods in his prose. Students objected to his 'aggressive punctuation style,' alleging that terminal punctuation was 'triggering and judgmental.' This represents the apotheosis of academic identity politics: where the mechanics of grammar itself become indices of ideological failure. The author's only crime was employing the humblest of punctuation marks as grammatical terminals—a practice apparently now considered an assault on sensibility. One can almost hear H.L. Mencken's ghost chuckling at the sheer absurdity of an institution so devoted to suppressing 'problematic' expression that it has now criminalized basic English syntax.
follyOfTheDay.commentary: The academy has finally found something to ban that doesn't require a court order: syntax.
